name: Carlyle Towers Condominiums
description: Carlyle Towers Condominiums is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 2 outdoor acrylic courts. The lines are overlaid on a tennis court, so the net is a little taller than a normal pickleball net. The courts are private. You can reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like lights.

name: Charles Houston Recreation Center
description: Charles Houston Recreation Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 3 indoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. The facility includes amenities like restrooms.

name: Chinquapin Tennis Pickleball Courts
description: Chinquapin Tennis Pickleball Courts is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 5 outdoor courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms, water, wheelchair accessibility, lights, and food.

name: Eugene Simpson Stadium Park
description: Eugene Simpson Stadium Park is a place to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 4 outdoor concrete courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. Reservations are not available. The courts are free. Players have access to restrooms and water.

name: Franconia Rec Center
description: Also has a gymnasium with temporary courts and nets. And 2 more outdoor courts with painted lines on top of tennis court lines. Bring your own net for these two extra outdoor courts.

name: George Washington Rec Center/Riverside Elementary School
description: George Washington Rec Center/Riverside Elementary School is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 10 outdoor asphalt courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. Reservations are not available. The facility includes amenities like wheelchair accessibility and restrooms. The courts are free.

name: Gum Springs Community Center
description: Gum Springs Community Center is one of the most popular places to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 2 indoor wood courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Amenities include lights.

name: Hollin Hills Pickleball & Tennis Club
description: Hollin Hills Pickleball & Tennis Club is a place to play pickleball in Alexandria, VA. There are 6 outdoor hard courts. These are dedicated courts with permanent lines and nets. You can reserve the courts. The facility includes amenities like restrooms and lights. A membership is required to play.
fees: Members only — One-time initiation fee of $400 for each new household membership.  Annual membership dues are set at $125 per adult over 18, $50 for children under 18.  $300 initiation fee for single

name: Hollin Meadows Swim & Tennis Club
description:
Courts 4 & 5 lined for tennis and pickleball.

Only smooth-soled tennis shoes are permitted on the courts. Bare feet, sandals, etc. are not permitted. 

Courts must be reserved through the online reservation system, and may be reserved only by members.

name: Martin Luther King Park
description: Come play pickleball at Martin Luther King Park in Alexandria, VA! There are 4 outdoor hard courts. The lines are permanent, and portable nets are available. The courts are free. Amenities include restrooms and water. The courts cannot be reserved.
